own_review_points: Dialect not registered in CommunityDatabase/CommunityDialectSelector - undiscoverable at runtime
datetimeFormat() Replacer order bug: ss->SS registered before SSSSSS->FFn, so fractional-second formats break
getFractionalSecondPrecisionInNanos returns 1_000L (a microsecond) with a 'nanoseconds' comment - value/comment mismatch
supportsFetchClause(ROWS_ONLY)=true but LimitHandler emits TOP ? - possible inconsistency
getResultSet override is dead code (not called) - human ref notes it but accepts keeping it
notes: Stronger entry: v8 found the missing dialect registration, a concrete and well-reasoned datetimeFormat replacement-ordering bug (with a corroborating cross-reference to OracleDialect), the 1_000L-vs-nanoseconds comment mismatch, and the missing @Override - four substantive findings. The fetch-clause/LimitHandler point is unverifiable from the truncation; defaultScrollMode and visibility points are trivia. Only modest overlap with the thin acceptance-stage human note (getResultSet being unused). Clearly net positive.
